title: Add M5 content schema and data-mod validation gate
summary: Implement the first M5 content validation gate and schema policy for anomaly roster, faction/governance hooks, season events and alpha catalog definitions without granting runtime code-mod authority.
acceptance: M5 content definitions for anomaly roster faction/governance hooks season events and alpha catalog entries have versioned schema validation and deterministic compile order
Data-mod inputs are validated for manifest shape path safety file size schema references semantics localization coverage and unsupported capabilities before runtime
Code mods network mods platform API mods executable scripts remote URLs unsafe paths recursive archive expansion and oversized assets fail closed with structured reasons
Compiled output records stable DefIndex ordering content manifest hash validation counters and failure diagnostics suitable for save/replay and Worker basis checks
Focused tests cover valid content invalid schema unknown references semantic failures missing localization unsafe paths unsupported capabilities and deterministic compile order

history.details: Created by WM-0072 M5 planning DAG; remains proposed until dependencies are done.

objective: Create the M5 data-only content validation gate before runtime M5 systems consume alpha content.
forbiddenPaths: code mods network mods platform API mods executable scripts
risksAndFileOwnership: Risk: schema work becomes runtime behavior; ownership is limited to content-schema, content-compiler and content-cli validation surfaces.
Risk: unsafe mod inputs reach runtime; every accepted input must have a structured validation report and fail-closed tests.
Risk: public schema changes sprawl; ADR-0010 must be cited and any dependency/package-manifest need blocks.
benchmarkImpact: Adds validation counters and compile-order evidence only; no runtime benchmark baseline update until WM-0083.
reviewRouting: Systems-architect completes to reviewer; simulation-engineer must be consulted before runtime owner stores consume compiled M5 defs.
rollbackModel: Revert M5 schema/compiler/CLI validation changes, tests, docs and report; leave M4 content validation behavior intact.
ownerGate: Reject unsafe paths unsupported capabilities and executable content before runtime
Record stable content manifest and DefIndex evidence
Block on package manifest dependency public protocol save schema or M6 needs

sparkEligibility.reason: Content schema and data-mod validation define public content boundaries and security policy, so they require systems-architect ownership.
